title: Tokens, Source Spans, Document Trees, Invariants, and Traversal · Runtime Lens: Node Model
summary: The parser concatenates HTML strings immediately, loses source locations, uses one mutable node class for every construct, and cannot report which input caused invalid nesting. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the node model increment in typed source-mapped document tree. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practi…
objectives: Model document, block, inline, text, code, link, image, and error nodes with explicit payload and children invariants.
Distinguish scanning, tokenization, block parsing, inline parsing, tree construction, transformation, validation, and rendering.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-node-model — Model document, block, inline, text, code, link, image, and error nodes with explicit payload and children invariants.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-document-ast-2
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-document-ast-2.
# Case 7417: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def worked_static_site_document_ast_2(tokens: int = 18, nodes: int = 15, source_spans: int = 15, invalid_edges: int = 0, max_depth: int = 5) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "readerworked2",
        "source7417",
        "parse2dekrow",
        "routeworked27417",
        "artifact7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if tokens < 1 or nodes < 1:
        return False, nodes, "tree-shape"
    if source_spans != nodes:
        return False, source_spans, "span-coverage"
    if invalid_edges > 0:
        return False, invalid_edges, "tree-invariant"
    if max_depth < 1 or max_depth > 64:
        return False, max_depth, "depth-budget"
    assert source_spans == nodes
    return True, nodes, "source-mapped-tree"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-node-model-1pfcbx
# Competency: ssg-node-model.
# Case 3525: a compromised-content recovery rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def evidence_ssg_node_model_1pfcbx(tokens: int = 18, nodes: int = 15, source_spans: int = 15, invalid_edges: int = 0, max_depth: int = 5) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1pfcbx = "1pfcbx-xbcfp1-3525"
    assert evidence_variant_1pfcbx
    evidence_axes_1pfcbx = (
        "reader1pfcbx",
        "source3525",
        "parsexbcfp1",
        "route1pfcbx3525",
        "artifact35251pfcbx",
        "recoveryxbcfp13525",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1pfcbx)) == 6
    if tokens < 1 or nodes < 1:
        return False, nodes, "tree-shape"
    if source_spans != nodes:
        return False, source_spans, "span-coverage"
    if invalid_edges > 0:
        return False, invalid_edges, "tree-invariant"
    if max_depth < 1 or max_depth > 64:
        return False, max_depth, "depth-budget"
    assert source_spans == nodes
    return True, nodes, "source-mapped-tree"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Node Model model · Theory Node Model
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Node Model: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 8191 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to construct a source-mapped acyclic document tree under a depth budget; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Model document, block, inline, text, code, link, image, and error nodes with explicit payload and children invariants.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Node Model model · Theory Node Model.
steps.why: Retrieving Node Model through the concrete typed source-mapped document tree case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Node Model model · Theory Node Model, keep Node Model connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Model document, block, inline, text, code, link, image, and error nodes with explicit payload and children invariants.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Node Model model · Theory Node Model, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Node Model precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Node Model model · Theory Node Model, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 8191 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to construct a source-mapped acyclic document tree under a depth budget; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Model document, block, inline, text, code, link, image, and error nodes with explicit payload and children invariants.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Node Model model · Theory Node Model, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: One generic node with nullable fields is the most flexible safe AST.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-node-model-1pfcbx.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-node-model-1pfcbx(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_node_model_1pfcbx\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?tokens < 1 or nodes < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?source_spans != nodes)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?invalid_edges > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?max_depth < 1 or max_depth > 64)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using model and document plus specific case evidence.
